known_limitations: AI move-explanation (/api/explain-move) requires a real Supabase JWT; cannot work anonymously — always errors. Server-side dependency, not fixable client-side.
Game Review over wss://api.chessr.io/ws?userId=anon connects without a token; whether the server treats anon as premium is server-side policy and remains unverified.

evidence: verified against v3.4.0: pre-unlock anchor occurrence counts identical to v3.3.2 original
unlocks.notes: Formalized this version (v3.4.0) — this stub was already applied in v3.3.2's shipped chessr-unlocked-all-features build but had never been captured as its own manifest entry.
unlocks.transform: fetchAccounts -> set {loading:false, accounts:[]}, no network call. linkAccount -> return {success:true}. unlinkAccount -> no-op. setNeedsLinking left as a plain setter (never triggered because of link-account-effect-noop).

evidence: verified against v3.4.0: pre-unlock anchor occurrence counts identical to v3.3.2 original (signUp: async x1, signIn: async x1)

locate.role: auth methods (pre-unlock locate anchors -- corrected in v3.4.0 pass, see notes)

unlocks.notes: locate.anchors corrected in the v3.4.0 pass: previously seeded with POST-unlock strings ('Sign-in is not required', 'No account system') which cannot locate the target in a fresh, not-yet-unlocked build. These pre-unlock anchors (the method names themselves) were verified present in both v3.3.2's original restoration and v3.4.0's fresh restoration before the unlock was applied.
unlocks.transform: Replace each method body with a stub returning the expected {success,error} shape; no Supabase or backend calls.

unlocks.description: Auth/plan store starts as a permanent premium anonymous session so the login gate never renders.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: plan: "free" x5, user: null x48, session: null x42 -- identical counts to v3.3.2 original

locate.role: auth store initial state (pre-unlock locate anchors -- corrected in v3.4.0 pass, see notes)

unlocks.notes: Non-null user is the hidden switch: `if (!user) return <LoginScreen/>` forces login while user is null. locate.anchors corrected in the v3.4.0 pass: previously seeded with POST-unlock strings ('id: "anon"', 'plan: "premium"') which cannot locate the target in a fresh, not-yet-unlocked build.
unlocks.transform: Set user/session to a mock anon object, plan:'premium', planLoading/initializing:false, guidelinesAcceptedAt:new Date(0).

unlocks.description: Version gate (/health minExtensionVersion) removed; never blocks UI load.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: minExtensionVersion x1, checkVersion: async x1 -- identical counts to v3.3.2 original

locate.role: version gate (pre-unlock locate anchors -- corrected in v3.4.0 pass, see notes)

unlocks.notes: locate.anchors corrected in the v3.4.0 pass: 'minExtensionVersion' only appears in the pre-unlock body (the /health fetch + semver compare), making it a strong pre-unlock-only anchor; it is absent after the transform.
unlocks.transform: Replace checkVersion body with unconditional {updateRequired:false, checking:false}; remove the /health fetch.

unlocks.description: linked: false / inGuild: null Discord integration store stubbed -- fetchStatus/fetchMembership/initLink/unlink are all no-network.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: pre-unlock anchor occurrence counts identical to v3.3.2 original
unlocks.notes: Formalized this version (v3.4.0) — this stub was already applied in v3.3.2's shipped chessr-unlocked-all-features build but had never been captured as its own manifest entry. v3.4.0's original endpoints use query-string params (/discord/status?userId=...) rather than path segments; wording-only difference, same neutralization.
unlocks.transform: fetchStatus -> set {linked:false, username:null, avatar:null, loading:false}, no network. fetchMembership -> set {inGuild:false}, no network. initLink -> no-op. unlink -> set {linked:false, username:null, avatar:null}, no network.

unlocks.description: Removed the two 'if (elo < 2500 && !limitStrength) setLimitStrength(true)' enforcement sites per bundle.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: exactly 2 sites present pre-unlock, identical to v3.3.2 original
unlocks.notes: Removal unlock: verify passes when the anchor is ABSENT from the unlocked build. This anchor is naturally pre-unlock-compatible (it's the enforcement code itself, not a post-unlock artifact) -- no correction needed.
unlocks.transform: Delete both enforcement blocks so manual ELO can exceed 2500.

unlocks.description: Engine settings initial state unlocked: no strength cap, unlimited search budget.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: limitStrength: true x1 -- identical to v3.3.2 original

locate.role: engine settings (pre-unlock locate anchor -- corrected in v3.4.0 pass, see notes)

unlocks.notes: locate.anchors corrected in the v3.4.0 pass: previously seeded with the POST-unlock value ('limitStrength: false'), which cannot locate the target in a fresh, not-yet-unlocked build. The pre-unlock value (true) is the correct locate anchor.

unlocks.description: Community Guidelines modal (gate: guidelinesAcceptedAt===null) suppressed by seeding a past date.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: guidelinesAcceptedAt: null x2 -- identical to v3.3.2 original

locate.role: guidelines gate (pre-unlock locate anchor -- corrected in v3.4.0 pass, see notes)

unlocks.notes: Second-order regression exposed once user became non-null (v3.3.2). locate.anchors corrected in the v3.4.0 pass: previously seeded with the POST-unlock value ('guidelinesAcceptedAt: new Date(0)'), which cannot locate the target in a fresh, not-yet-unlocked build.
unlocks.transform: Initial state guidelinesAcceptedAt:new Date(0) so the modal's null-gate is never true.

unlocks.description: Neutralized the 'detect chess profile -> setNeedsLinking(true)' effect so the link-account nag never shows.
evidence: verified against v3.4.0: effect present pre-unlock at the same structural shape (platform detection -> profile lookup -> conditional linking-flag setter) as v3.3.2 original
unlocks.notes: Removal at an OBFUSCATED, aliased call site (original calls the store setter via an obfuscated alias, not the readable name), so grep-anchoring is unreliable — the readable anchor is absent in BOTH builds, in every version processed so far. Detection must be confirmed by the model against the previous version's effect body (recovery-ladder step 2). Anchor reliability capped at medium.
unlocks.transform: Replace the effect body with an unconditional early return.
